architecture.summary: OVM trains a value model using outcome-level supervision to guide LLM decoding on mathematical reasoning tasks. The system scores partial reasoning paths by their potential to reach correct final answers, rather than by step-level correctness. During inference, the value model steers token-by-token decoding to prioritize trajectories leading to correct conclusions. Information flows from reasoning steps → value model scorer → guidance signals → LLM decoder.

biblio.title: OVM, Outcome-supervised Value Models for Planning in Mathematical Reasoning

claims.statement: OVM-7B achieves state-of-the-art results among LLMs up to 13B parameters on GSM8K
OVM eliminates the need for labor-intensive annotations of step-level correctness, thereby significantly enhancing its scalability
assessing the potential of an incomplete reasoning path can be more advantageous than simply ensuring per-step correctness, as the former approach leads towards a correct final answer
OVM does not utilize GPT-4 or code execution
outcome supervision for guided decoding essentially acts as a value model
experiments on two multi-step mathematical reasoning datasets, GSM8K and Game of 24, demonstrate the superior performance of the OVM model
